hook: The AI gave you a 7-step framework when you just needed a straight answer. Here's why.
caption:
The AI gave you a 7-step framework when you just needed a straight answer. Here's why.

AI doesn't know if you're a "just tell me what to do" person or a "walk me through the reasoning" person.

So it defaults to... both. At the same time. Which satisfies nobody.

Here's what's actually happening:

Generic AI is optimizing for the average user. The person who might want context. Or bullet points. Or a numbered list. Or a caveat. Or three caveats.

But you're not the average user. You have a thinking style.

Maybe you process visually and walls of text make your brain shut off.
Maybe you need the "why" before the "how" or nothing sticks.
Maybe you're a bottom-line-first person who reads the last paragraph of every article first (no judgment — same).

When AI doesn't know any of this, it hedges. It over-explains. It gives you a buffet when you ordered a sandwich.

BootFile fixes this by building a profile of how YOU actually think — then every response is calibrated to match it.

No more re-prompting. No more "can you make this shorter." No more framework soup.

Just answers that feel like they were written for your brain.

Because they were.
